pages.summary:
There are three core algorithms behind recommendation systems:
Collaborative Filtering Content-Based Filtering Hybrid Approach Filtering Collaborative Filtering Collaborative filtering algorithm attempts to predict what a user will like based on the patterns from other users, hence the name. It does not factor in the item content.
The advantage of collaborative filtering is that it can find more hidden patterns.
Why does collaborative filtering able to find hidden patterns better? Collaborative filtering works better because of co-occurrence. (Co-occurrence is basically two events occurring more when together) This allows for subconscious patterns and nuances to be recognized by the model that typical genres and classes cannot analyze. Even within collaborative filtering we have more approaches.
pages.title: Recommendation Systems

pages.summary:
GitHub Repository: https://github.com/enerhim/neuralnetworkc This was a learning project of mine for learning the implementation of nns using vectorized gradient descent and backpropagation.
For the MNIST Handwritten Digits, the layer structure: {784, 256, 128, 10}, alpha = 0.1 w/ decay, epochs = 1000 over 60k images (full batch gradient descent): The model achieved 93.20% accuracy on 10k test images. With almost the same structure and hyperparameters as above, the MNIST Fashion Dataset acheived 84.15% accuracy on 10k test images. A larger hidden layer structure will likely lead to much better performance due to higher complexity of images despite them being the same size.
pages.title: neuralnetworkc
